headline: Agentic and multi-step AI techniques dominate the quarter, with orchestration primitives breaking out across the field.
body: The quarter's defining shift is the broad rotation into agentic AI and AI agents, now joined by a cluster of new entrants that fill in the underlying stack: multi-model workflows, prompt chaining, tool calling, and task decomposition are all breaking out together, signaling that practitioners are moving from single-model prompting toward structured, multi-step orchestration. Code generation and AI-assisted coding are simultaneous breakouts, with GitHub Copilot's new usage-based pricing drawing sharp user reaction [6] and Simon Willison's pasted-file editor surfacing concrete tooling patterns at the coding layer [3][4]. On the model and infrastructure side, NVIDIA's Cosmos 3 and Nemotron 3 Ultra arrived alongside the RTX Spark [5], Mellum2 went open source as a fast inference-optimized model [0], and speculation around an Anthropic IPO at a trillion-dollar valuation and a GPT-5.6 release kept frontier model momentum high [1][2]. Colorado's rollback of its landmark AI governance law [7] marks a notable policy reversal, keeping the regulatory environment unsettled as capability deployment accelerates.
